info.title: DogFoodAndFun Approval API
info.description: Localhost-only sidecar for parallel web/Telegram approvals.

get.description:
Return one summary per folder under campaigns_dir that has a config.

Folders without ``campaign_config.json`` are silently skipped — the
UI only renders runnable campaigns. Invalid configs are also skipped
(logged at WARN) so a single broken JSON file can't 500 the list.
Return full detail (summary + history) for a single campaign.

post.description:
Spawn ``scripts.publish_campaign`` as a detached subprocess.

Fire-and-forget by design: the publisher writes to ``state.json`` and
the frontend polls ``GET /campaigns``. Lock conflicts surface there
as ``last_status="error"`` on the next poll, identical to the cron
path. Returns immediately with the spawned PID.
Accept a WordPress publish event and queue PDF generation.

schema.title: Response Recipe Card Webhook Api V1 Webhooks Recipe Card Post

get.description: List stored recipes (alphabetical) with optional filters.

parameters.name: season

schema.description: filter to recipes in-season for this season
schema.title: Season

parameters.description: filter to recipes in-season for this season

get.description: Aggregated publish outcomes across all recipes (phase 10, local log).

post.description: Promote a pending recipe to approved (the human gate, phase 5).
Reject a pending recipe so it is never published (phase 5).

get.description: Full detail for one recipe, including ingredients and steps.

Render the recipe as a standalone HTML page (DB fields + image artifacts).

Image refs point at the artifact endpoint so photos resolve inside an
``<iframe>`` preview. Re-rendered per call to reflect current DB state — the
same markup the publisher emits, viewable before anything goes live.

Render the post_image.html recipe card with base64-embedded assets.

The card shows: food photo | dog avatar | recipe name | ingredients | timing.
All assets are inlined as base64 data URIs so the page is fully self-contained.
Returns a green placeholder when ``post_image.jpg`` has not yet been generated.

post.description:
Refresh each recipe's publish status from the publish records.

Reads the brand's ``campaigns/**/metadata.json`` and
``published_recipes.json`` and writes per-channel status onto matching
recipe rows. Idempotent and safe to call repeatedly.

get.description: List every file in the recipe's artifact folder, for the UI viewer.
Serve a single artifact file (path-traversal guarded).

parameters.name: path

schema.description: path relative to the artifact folder
schema.title: Path

parameters.description: path relative to the artifact folder

get.description:
Serve a media file referenced by a recipe's media manifest.

Paths are BRAND_DIR-relative and may live under either the live
``recipe_artifacts`` folder or the ``_migrated_backup`` folder, so this
guards to *both* of the recipe's own media dirs (path-traversal safe) rather
than the single artifacts dir used by ``get_artifact``.

parameters.name: path

schema.description: BRAND_DIR-relative media path from the manifest
schema.title: Path

parameters.description: BRAND_DIR-relative media path from the manifest

get.description: Generate and return a story card JPEG for the given recipe.
Most-recent-first published posts/comments, optionally filtered, plus counts.
List content ideas, newest first. Defaults to all statuses.
Return slide URLs for an idea's saved carousel (empty list if not generated yet).

schema.title: Response List Idea Slides Api V1 Ideas  Idea Id  Slides Get

get.description: Serve a single carousel slide JPEG.

patch.description: Update the status of a single idea.

schema.title: Response Update Idea Status Api V1 Ideas  Idea Id  Status Patch

get.description: List TikTok follow candidates.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: pending, followed, skipped
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, followed, skipped

patch.description: Update the status of a TikTok candidate.

schema.title: Response Update Candidate Status Api V1 Tiktok Candidates  Handle  Status Patch

get.description: Returns the current site configuration.
All blog-post pairs, group-join candidates, ideas, seeds, campaign-verify items, and comments awaiting a decision.
Tail of ``logs/engagement_log.jsonl``, most recent first.
Look up a single item by id.

schema.title: Response Get Item Api V1 Items  Item Id  Get

post.description: Approve an item. Dispatches on type.
Reject an item. Dispatches on type. Logs ``body.reason`` (free-form).
Approve with edited content. Only valid for blog_post items.

get.description:
List all Facebook groups bucketed by status.

Merges two sources:
  - groups_tracker.json -> status in {joined, join_requested, rejected}
  - pending_groups.json -> projected with synthetic status="not_joined_yet"

put.description: Update a Facebook group's status / posting_mode in the groups DB.

get.description: Liveness probe for launchd / curl. 204 = OK, no body needed.
List all scheduled workers with their last run status from DB.

schema.title: Response List Workers Api V1 Workers Get

get.description: Return the last run status for a single worker.

post.description:
Fire a scheduled worker on demand.

``body.count`` (1–3) spawns that many parallel instances — useful once
workers are backed by a Redis task queue so each instance independently
pops and processes tasks.  Each instance gets its own PID file
``<suffix>_<i>.pid``.  If any slot is already occupied the whole
request is rejected with 409.

get.description: Return scheduled flows defined in schedule.json that aren't loaded in launchctl.
Return the last N lines of the log file for a scheduled job.
Return the JSON content of the output_file for a scheduled job.

schema.title: Response Get Schedule Artifact Api V1 Workers  Label  Artifact Get

ActivityEntry.title: ActivityEntry
ActivityEntry.description:
One row in ``logs/engagement_log.jsonl`` as exposed by ``/activity``.

Some legacy rows in the JSONL file use ``action="group_join_request"``;
the API layer normalises those to ``"group_join"`` before constructing
this model so the frontend only ever sees the canonical value. Older
rows may also lack the ``date`` field — ``lib.activity_log`` derives
it from ``timestamp`` so the UI can rely on both being present.

AffiliateProduct.title: AffiliateProduct
AffiliateProduct.description: One matched Amazon-Associates product (from the affiliate-matching phase).

AnalyticsResponse.title: AnalyticsResponse
AnalyticsResponse.description: Aggregated publish outcomes (phase 10, local outcome log).

ApproveBody.title: ApproveBody
ApproveBody.description:
Body for ``POST /items/{id}/approve``.

All fields optional: a bare approve just commits the existing draft.
Pass ``text`` to override a comment, or ``fb_caption``/``ig_caption``
to override a blog post pair before approval.

ArtifactItem.title: ArtifactItem
ArtifactItem.description: One file in a recipe's artifact folder, listed for the UI.

BlogPostItem.title: BlogPostItem
BlogPostItem.description:
A WordPress post awaiting FB + IG caption sign-off.

``channel`` is the per-platform fan-out decision set by the approver:

- Web UI: ``POST /items/{id}/approve?channel=fb_only`` stamps this field
  before flipping ``status`` to ``approved``.
- Telegram: the reply parser in ``lib/notifier.py`` maps text replies
  (``approve``/``fb``/``ig``) onto ``"both"``/``"fb_only"``/``"ig_only"``.

The publisher (``scripts/content_pipeline.py::stage_publish``) reads
``channel`` after ``send_and_wait`` returns to decide which platforms to
actually post to. ``None`` is valid only while the item is still pending
or was skipped.

CampaignDetail.title: CampaignDetail
CampaignDetail.description:
Detail payload for ``GET /api/v1/campaigns/{name}``.

Extends ``CampaignSummary`` with the full run history list so the UI
can render the timeline view without a second round-trip.

CampaignSummary.title: CampaignSummary
CampaignSummary.description:
Summary row for ``GET /api/v1/campaigns``.

Aggregates the on-disk campaign config + state.json + ready/published
folder counts into a single UI-friendly shape. ``last_status`` is
derived from ``state.history[-1].status`` and defaults to ``"never"``
when the campaign has no run history yet.

CampaignVerifyItem.title: CampaignVerifyItem
CampaignVerifyItem.description: A campaign awaiting final human verification before publish.

CommentItem.title: CommentItem
CommentItem.description:
A Facebook / Instagram / WordPress engagement comment.

Legacy schema — engagement comments no longer flow through the web UI,
but the model is retained so the autonomous pipeline (comment_poster)
and legacy items in ``comment_queue.json`` can still validate.

EditBody.title: EditBody
EditBody.description:
Body for ``POST /items/{id}/edit``.

Either ``text`` (comment items) or one/both of ``fb_caption`` /
``ig_caption`` (blog-post items) must be set — enforced in the route
handler so the response is a clean 422 instead of an opaque ValidationError.

GroupItem.title: GroupItem
GroupItem.description:
A Facebook group surfaced by ``fb_group_scout`` awaiting a join
decision.

Producer side (``lib/group_discovery/state.py:add_to_pending``) writes
the discovery payload without ``id``/``status``/``decided_by`` — the API
layer synthesises those when reading (see ``lib/groups_queue.py``). The
``model_config = extra="allow"`` inherited from ``_ItemBase`` keeps the
extra producer fields (``post_frequency``, ``competitor_names``,
``description``) intact for the UI even though they aren't declared.

IdeaItem.title: IdeaItem
IdeaItem.description: A recipe/content idea from the ideator queue awaiting approval.

MissingFlowEntry.title: MissingFlowEntry
MissingFlowEntry.description: One scheduled flow that's defined in schedule.json but not loaded in launchctl.

PublishChannel.title: PublishChannel
PublishChannel.description:
Publish state for one channel (wp / pdf / ig / fb).

IG-only extras (``caption`` / ``reel_url`` / ``post_url``) carry the
Instagram single-image post + reel split surfaced in the UI popup. They
stay empty for the other channels.

RecipeIngredient.title: RecipeIngredient
RecipeIngredient.description: A single ingredient line from a scraped recipe.

RecipeMedia.title: RecipeMedia
RecipeMedia.description:
On-disk media for a recipe, as BRAND_DIR-relative paths.

Sourced from the row's ``generated_content.media`` manifest (links only —
the bytes stay on disk). The UI turns each path into a serving URL via the
``/recipes/{id}/media-file`` endpoint.

RecipeSummary.title: RecipeSummary
RecipeSummary.description: List-row view of a stored recipe (no ingredients/steps).

SeedItem.title: SeedItem
SeedItem.description: A recipe seed awaiting approval before full campaign generation.
